But I found the pauses, in weird spots, when Jesse Plemons was speaking a bit distracting. Overall the dialogs and messages behind them were mostly excellent, but IMO,  their effectiveness was diluted by muddled side plots. *Spoiler alerts follow!!! Bringing in Connie Britton as Mullen’s ex-sidepiece dropped my overall satisfaction level a lot. She can’t act. And she looks all of 60 years old, too old to have a 12 year old child. And although I can usually ignore actors weird physical features, I can’t ignore Britton’s extremely low neanderthal-like hairline. Her character is supposed to be a senior citizen but also has dreams of being appointed as a judge and has public appearances. Her character would probably do a little something to look better when out in public, like applying some eyebrows and lipstick. I’m saying that as a woman who’s at least 5 years older than Joan Allen, and who believes in trying to look good without going to extremes like plastic surgery.
